body.title: ADC scheduling primitive — move Igor timing/scheduling into shared device tool
body.result: Superseded by T-nanny-ogg-device — Nanny Ogg device is the full design including scheduling + world-interaction dispatch + agent registry. Decision D-granny-nanny-2026-05-28.
body.description:
Igor already has a 'when to do a thing' component (TIMING_TIER on push sources, experiment_scheduler, sleep_clock). Cherny's Routines concept maps to this: server-side persistent scheduled tasks that survive laptop close. Abstract this into agent_datacenter as a shared scheduling tool any device can use — Igor becomes one consumer, not the only owner.

Motivation: ADC is the portable erector set; Igor is one device. The scheduling primitive belongs in ADC so future devices (Librarian, auditor, etc.) can also have scheduled behaviors without reimplementing Igor's pattern.

Design questions to answer:
- What is the minimal interface? `schedule(device_id, task, interval_sec, condition?)` or richer?
- Persistent (Postgres) vs ephemeral (in-process)? Likely Postgres for survive-restart semantics.
- How does a device register its scheduled tasks? Static at init vs. dynamic API?
- Does this replace Igor's TIMING_TIER or sit alongside it?

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery/design step. Likely agent_datacenter/devices/scheduling_device/ or agent_datacenter/tools/scheduler.py + Igor push_sources.py migration
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, postgres-for-state, device-inherits-BaseDevice if OOP
**Scope boundary:** design the interface + migration path; implementation is a follow-on sprint. Out of scope: changing Igor's existing TIMING_TIER until design is approved.
**Test plan:** design ticket — test plan written in follow-on implementation ticket
